input.initialPrompt: Analyze this project for incremental build issues. Identify any custom targets that would unnecessarily re-run on every build even when nothing has changed.

data.reasoningText: Let me analyze the project files to identify incremental build issues.

toolResult.textResultForLlm:
1. <Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
2.   <PropertyGroup>
3.     <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
4.   </PropertyGroup>
5. 
6.   <Target Name="GenerateTimestamp" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
7.     <PropertyGroup>
8.       <TimestampFile>$(IntermediateOutputPath)build-timestamp.txt</TimestampFile>
9.     </PropertyGroup>
10.     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(TimestampFile)" Lines="Built at: $([System.DateTime]::Now.ToString())" Overwrite="true" />
11.   </Target>
12. 
13.   <Target Name="EmbedGitHash" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
14.     <Exec Command="git rev-parse HEAD" ConsoleToMsBuild="true" IgnoreExitCode="true">
15.       <Output TaskParameter="ConsoleOutput" PropertyName="GitHash" />
16.     </Exec>
17.     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs"
18.                      Lines="namespace IncrementalBroken { internal static class BuildInfo { public const string GitHash = "$(GitHash)"%3B } }"
19.                      Overwrite="true" />
20.     <ItemGroup>
21.       <Compile Include="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs" />
22.     </ItemGroup>
23.   </Target>
24. </Project>
25.

data.content:
## Incremental Build Issues Found

There are **two custom targets** that break incremental builds:

### 1. `GenerateTimestamp` (line 6–11)
- Writes the current date/time to `build-timestamp.txt` **every build**.
- Since `$([System.DateTime]::Now)` always produces a new value, the file always changes, which invalidates downstream caching.
- **Fix:** Add `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes to skip the target when outputs are up-to-date, or remove the timestamp entirely (timestamps are inherently non-deterministic).

### 2. `EmbedGitHash` (line 13–23)
- Runs `git rev-parse HEAD` and writes `git-hash.g.cs` **unconditionally** on every build.
- Even if the git hash hasn't changed, the target has no `Inputs`/`Outputs` so MSBuild always re-executes it. The rewritten `.g.cs` file then forces `CoreCompile` to re-run.
- **Fix:** Add `Inputs="$(MSBuildAllProjects)"` and `Outputs="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs"`, or use a hash-comparison step so the file is only rewritten when the content actually changes.

### Summary

| Target | Root Cause | Impact |
|--------|-----------|--------|
| `GenerateTimestamp` | Always writes a new value (DateTime.Now) | Invalidates incremental compile every time |
| `EmbedGitHash` | No `Inputs`/`Outputs`; file rewritten unconditionally | Forces recompilation even when HEAD hasn't changed |

Both targets lack the MSBuild incrementality contract (`Inputs`/`Outputs`), causing a full recompile on every `dotnet build` regardless of source changes.
